questions.question: In the early medieval India, which of the following refers to tax levied upon the whole village?
questions.explanation: The land-grants of the early medieval period mention Bhaga, Bhoga, Kara and Hiranya as the chief sources of revenue to the donees. Pindaka was a tax which was levied upon the whole village.

questions.question: Which of the following refers to a fine realised for 10 offences committed by the villagers?
questions.explanation: Dasaparadha refers to a fine which was realised for 10 offences committed by the villagers. Those offences included disobedience to the orders of the king, murder of a woman, intermixture of castes, adultery, theft, obscenity, assault, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following probably meant extra cess over and above the fixed revenue of the state during the early medieval period?
questions.explanation: Udranga also called as Upaikara probably meant extra cess over and above the fixed revenue of the state during the early medieval period whereas Dasaparadha was a fine which was collected for 10 offences committed by the villagers.

questions.question: Hiranya refers to which of the following form of tax during early medieval India?

options.label: A
options.text: Fine realized for 10 offences committed by the villagers.

options.label: B
options.text: A lumpsum assessment in cash upon villages as distinguished from the grain share of the king assessed upon the individual cultivators.

options.label: C
options.text: A periodical tax levied primarily upon agricultural land over and above the king\'s normal share of the grain.

answer.label: B
answer.text: A lumpsum assessment in cash upon villages as distinguished from the grain share of the king assessed upon the individual cultivators.

questions.explanation: Hiranya during the medieval times referred to lumpsum assessment in cash upon villages as distinguished from the grain share of the king assessed upon the individual cultivators.

questions.question: Which of the following forms of industry were in vogue during the early medieval period?1. A great variety and different qualities of textiles were produced.2. Surashtra was famous for bell-metal and Vanga for tin industry.3. The art of the jeweller appears to have maintained its old level.Select the correct option from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: According to the record of the early medieval period a great variety and different qualities of textiles were produced. The Surashtra region was famous for bell-metal and Vanga for tin industry. However, the art of the jeweller appears to have maintained its old level.
